medications.indication_rationale: A GP decision for subclinical hypothyroidism after monitoring; not an acute telehealth prescription tonight.
Not appropriate to start via telehealth for a self-limiting panic attack.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Do box/paced breathing and 5-4-3-2-1 grounding, close the research tabs, and stop searching tonight.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Reverses hyperventilation and terminates the panic cycle.

patient_education_points.point: Your racing heart, fast breathing and weakness are a panic attack from reading a scary-sounding result. Your pulse is regular and you have no chest pain — your heart and lungs are fine and your body is not shutting down.

Subclinical Hashimoto's (a raised TSH with a normal T4) is a slow, mild condition that is simply monitored — not an overnight emergency. Do slow breathing, stop the online research, and see your GP next week for thyroid blood tests.
Go to ED / call 000 if you get crushing chest pain spreading to your arm/jaw, sharp pain on breathing with a swollen calf, your pulse becomes very fast or irregular, or you faint.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: TFTs and thyroid monitoring; health-anxiety support.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Chest pain, pleuritic pain with leg swelling, fast/irregular pulse, or syncope — ED/000.

must_include_items: Screen a dangerous arrhythmia, PE and ACS before reassuring
Confidently de-escalate a low-risk panic attack
errors_of_commission: Reflex ambulance/ED for a regular-rate panic attack
Telling her her body is shutting down
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: T2 case with a bidirectional trap: passing requires BOTH an arrhythmia/PE/ACS screen AND confident non-escalation of the low-risk panic attack.
